verses.1: The LORD’s word came to me, saying,
verses.2: “Go and proclaim in the ears of Jerusalem, saying, ‘The LORD says,
verses.3: Israel was holiness to the LORD,
verses.4: Hear the LORD’s word, O house of Jacob, and all the families of the house of Israel!
verses.6: They didn’t say, ‘Where is the LORD who brought us up out of the land of Egypt,
verses.7: I brought you into a plentiful land
verses.8: The priests didn’t say, ‘Where is the LORD?’
verses.9: “Therefore I will yet contend with you,” says the LORD,
verses.10: For pass over to the islands of Kittim, and see.
verses.11: Has a nation changed its gods,
verses.13: “For my people have committed two evils:
verses.15: The young lions have roared at him and raised their voices.
verses.16: The children also of Memphis and Tahpanhes have broken the crown of your head.
verses.18: Now what do you gain by going to Egypt, to drink the waters of the Shihor?
verses.20: “For long ago I broke off your yoke,
verses.21: Yet I had planted you a noble vine,
verses.22: For though you wash yourself with lye,
verses.23: “How can you say, ‘I am not defiled.
verses.24: a wild donkey used to the wilderness, that sniffs the wind in her craving.
verses.26: As the thief is ashamed when he is found,
verses.27: who tell wood, ‘You are my father,’
verses.28: “But where are your gods that you have made for yourselves?
verses.30: “I have struck your children in vain.
verses.33: How well you prepare your way to seek love!
verses.34: Also the blood of the souls of the innocent poor is found in your skirts.
verses.36: Why do you go about so much to change your ways?
verses.37: You will also leave that place with your hands on your head;
